content: We can reframe parallel orchestration and ingestion systems built with reader agents as a colony of biological pathogenic agents, where each individual reader agent acts as a distinct biological agent that targets a specific 'host' data source to 'infect' it by extracting information in parallel. This collective distributed action lets the full system ingest far more unstructured data at scale than a sequential system, just as a collective of biological agents spreads faster than a single pathogen.
source_topics: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
trigger_topic: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
